title: Good Friday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## April 7, 2023 | Sermon: "Good Friday" | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

April 7, 2023 was Good Friday itself, the central observance of Holy Week in the Christian calendar, falling within the season of Lent. The primary liturgical reading for Good Friday comes from the Passion narrative (John 18:1-19:42 in the RCL), accompanied by the Solemn Collects and typically the Veneration of the Cross. The sermon would have been preached during the traditional Good Friday liturgy, likely in the morning or afternoon, with no Eucharist celebrated on this day in traditional Anglican practice.

---

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of its 80th General Convention (held in July 2022), which had approved full inclusion of LGBTQ+ persons in all orders of ministry and marriage rites. Bishop Kym Harris of the Episcopal Diocese of Colorado was actively engaged in implementing these resolutions and navigating their reception across the Rocky Mountain region. No major Episcopal leadership changes or controversies were dominating headlines in this specific week, though debates over the recent General Convention actions remained live in many parishes.

---

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver and Colorado were experiencing early spring weather in early April 2023, with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit after a harsh winter. The Colorado legislature was in its final weeks of the 2023 session (ending May 10), with education funding and water rights dominating discussion. No major local disasters or crises were affecting the Capitol Hill neighborhood or downtown Denver area in the week immediately before April 7, though the city remained focused on ongoing homelessness and housing affordability issues.

---

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The U.S. was gripped by the aftermath of the Idaho college murders case, with Bryan Kohberger's trial underway in Moscow, Idaho (jury selection had begun in late March). The debt ceiling crisis was intensifying, with negotiations between President Biden and House Speaker Kevin McCarthy ongoing over raising or suspending the debt limit. Bank instability remained a concern following the March collapses of Silicon Valley Bank and Signature Bank, though markets had largely stabilized by early April.

---

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine was entering its second year with Russian forces conducting renewed offensives in eastern Ukraine; Bakhmut had recently fallen to Russian forces on May 20, 2023 (note: this is after April 7, so in early April the city was still contested). Turkey and Syria were in the early stages of recovery from the catastrophic February earthquakes that killed over 50,000 people, with ongoing aftershocks and humanitarian efforts. China was emerging from strict COVID lockdowns and beginning to normalize travel and economic activity after years of zero-COVID policy.

---

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in early April 2023 was experiencing a moment of celebrity drama and late-stage award season, with the Oscars controversy from March (Will Smith slapping Chris Rock) still being relitigated and discussed. The Denver Nuggets were in the midst of their championship NBA season (they would win in June 2023), creating local sports enthusiasm and pride. There was a broader sense of economic anxiety and institutional fragility in the U.S., with bank failures and political dysfunction creating an undercurrent of concern, making the themes of suffering, mortality, and redemption in a Good Friday sermon particularly resonant.
